  "clarification_question": "To focus feedback on this abstract, which primary track should I take? Choose one: (A) high-level argument and contribution framing; (B) novelty and claim-strength assessment versus prior hybrid/routing work; (C) sentence-level editing/polish; (D) risk/overclaim audit (uncertainty, scope, downstream use).",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "High-level conceptual framing is central because the abstract advances a specific contribution claim about learned per-query routing for climate emulation.",
    "R1.2": "Checking logical consistency is important to see whether the premises about heterogeneity, router design, and inputs support the compute-savings and robustness conclusions.",
    "R1.4": "Novelty assessment is central given the explicit 'first work' claim relative to prior static-partition hybrid systems.",
    "R1.6": "Sentence-level clarity and concision can materially affect how the contribution and evidence are perceived in an abstract.",
    "RX.1": "Any feedback should note uncertainties and limits inherent in claims about robustness under non-stationarity.",
    "RX.2": "Overclaim avoidance is critical because the text includes strong expectations about generalization and state-of-the-art advancement.",
    "RX.3": "Scope adherence ensures comments stay within the provided abstract rather than speculating about unseen sections or data.",
    "RX.4": "Downstream-harm avoidance matters since precipitation forecasts inform flood and water-resource decisions and overconfidence could mislead users.",
    "RX.5": "Provenance and traceability require tying feedback to specific statements in the provided excerpt."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: To focus feedback on this abstract, which primary track should I take? Choose one: (A) high-level argument and contribution framing; (B) novelty and claim-strength assessment versus prior hybrid/routing work; (C) sentence-level editing/polish; (D) risk/overclaim audit (uncertainty, scope, downstream use).

rationale.R1.1: High-level conceptual framing is central because the abstract advances a specific contribution claim about learned per-query routing for climate emulation.
rationale.R1.2: Checking logical consistency is important to see whether the premises about heterogeneity, router design, and inputs support the compute-savings and robustness conclusions.
rationale.R1.4: Novelty assessment is central given the explicit 'first work' claim relative to prior static-partition hybrid systems.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence-level clarity and concision can materially affect how the contribution and evidence are perceived in an abstract.
rationale.RX.1: Any feedback should note uncertainties and limits inherent in claims about robustness under non-stationarity.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is critical because the text includes strong expectations about generalization and state-of-the-art advancement.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence ensures comments stay within the provided abstract rather than speculating about unseen sections or data.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance matters since precipitation forecasts inform flood and water-resource decisions and overconfidence could mislead users.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability require tying feedback to specific statements in the provided excerpt.
